findings.detail: Description of "classify_api_fields" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Inventory the PII, secret, and sensitive fields an OpenAPI exposes, across parameters and request/response schemas. Run this before exposing an API to agents.

name: find_related
description: Given any doc (type + slug), the most related content across the whole network, ranked by shared-tag relevance (rare, specific tags weigh most). "More like this," across stories, areas, guidance, papers, conversations, and more.

name: get_stats
description: PRO — catalog intelligence: per-type counts, stories-per-year, and the top tags by reach across the network.

name: find_company_insights
description: Browse profiled companies (Fortune 1000 + API providers) by name; ranked by technology-readiness signal. Free.

name: insights_dimensions
description: Cross-section ranking of the 40 tracked investment dimensions (AI, cloud, governance, …) by company signal. Free.

name: insights_adoption
description: Services, tools, or standards ranked by how many companies adopt them. Free.

name: get_company_insight
description: Full demand-side profile for one company: 40-dimension readiness, adopted stack, per-quarter history. Pro.

name: company_gaps
description: A company's weakest investment dimensions — where it is thin. Pro.

name: validate_api
description: Lint an OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, Arazzo, or JSON Schema document against the curated best-of-breed ruleset (Spectral) and return the findings. Pass your own `ruleset` to run rules you own instead of the catalog — which is what you should do before gating on anything.

name: api_coverage
description: Measure how much of an API description your governance actually inspects — coverage by section, each rule's reach, and the rules that match nothing. Like test coverage, one altitude up.

name: reconcile_waivers
description: Reconcile a waivers file against lint output: suppress sanctioned violations and surface the expired, stale, and expiring ones. Returns the effective failing set a build should gate on.

name: governance_report
description: Turn Spectral findings into a self-contained HTML governance report — severity summary, grouped by rule, framed toward progress.

name: verify_certificate
description: Re-verify a governance certificate against the API it attests to. If the API drifted or the certificate was forged, verification fails.

name: classify_api_fields
description: Inventory the PII, secret, and sensitive fields an OpenAPI exposes, across parameters and request/response schemas. Run this before exposing an API to agents.

name: diff_api_versions
description: Compare two OpenAPI versions and classify every change as breaking, non-breaking, or an addition — at path, operation, parameter, request-body, and response granularity.

name: api_scorecard
description: Score an API's maturity 0-100 with a letter grade across design, governance coverage, documentation, and (from an apis.json) discoverability, agent-readiness, and operations. Pass your own `ruleset` — a score against rules you never adopted means little.

name: govern_estate
description: PRO — Run the core governance checks across a WHOLE SET of internal APIs and roll them up: per-API score/grade/errors/PII, plus estate aggregates (avg score, grade distribution, APIs with PII/secrets) and a worst-first punch-list of what to fix. Pass `apis` ([{id, document}]) and your own `ruleset`.

name: deprecation_posture
description: Report every deprecated operation and whether it actually announces a sunset. The ones that go quiet are the ones that break consumers.

name: validate_onboarding
description: Validate an API Onboarding Descriptor (AID) against the api-commons schema and report which optional sections it fills in.

name: certify_api
description: Issue a tamper-evident governance certificate attesting an API passed a named ruleset at a profile threshold. A SHA-256 fingerprint lets anyone re-verify it.

name: export_agent_rules
description: Turn a governance ruleset into agent-native guidance — an AGENTS.md block, a system-prompt set, a per-rule remediation pack, and a digest — so agents follow the rules while authoring, not after linting.

name: audit_pipeline
description: Score a repository's Spectral CI setup against an 8-point maturity rubric and return a prioritized punch-list. Pass a public `repo` ("owner/name") or `files`.

name: forge_mcp_tools
description: From an OpenAPI, forge an MCP server design, per-operation tools, Agent Skills, an enriched OpenAPI, a server.json, and apis.json properties.

name: gate_agent_context
description: Choose which operations and fields an API exposes to agents and emit a governed Tyk OpenAPI, an MCP tool manifest, and a Spectral ruleset with PII and secret checks.

name: apply_overlay
description: Apply an OpenAPI Overlay to a document and report what each action matched. Use it for deprecation and migration choreography without forking the contract.

name: mock_payloads
description: Generate an example request and success-response payload for every operation from its schema.

name: code_snippets
description: Generate runnable curl, JavaScript, and Python samples for every operation, with path params filled in and a realistic body.

name: score_reusability
description: Score how reusable a set of APIs is and detect duplication across them — the anti-sprawl view of an estate.

name: map_api_experience
description: Map each REST operation to its MCP tool and Agent Skill — the API -> MCP -> Agent-Skill chain — from an APIs.json, with a free/paid coverage scorecard and a flow map.

name: generate_agent_descriptor
description: Generate an API's llms.txt and an AGENTS.md consumption contract — base URL, auth, the operation table, deprecated operations to avoid, and usage rules.

evidence.snippet: Inventory the PII, secrets, and sensitive fields in an OpenAPI — what an agent must not see.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_contact, search_api_evangelist, find_posts, get_post, get_paper, get_video, get_feeds · Private data: get_contact, validate_api, reconcile_waivers, classify_api_fields, govern_estate, audit_pipeline, gate_agent_context, apply_overlay · Public sink: get_contact, get_post · Destructive: validate_api, classify_api_fields, govern_estate.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_contact, search_api_evangelist, find_posts, get_post, get_paper, get_video, get_feeds · Private data: get_contact, validate_api, reconcile_waivers, classify_api_fields, govern_estate, audit_pipeline, gate_agent_context, apply_overlay · Public sink: get_contact, get_post · Destructive: validate_api, classify_api_fields, govern_estate

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
